summary: Study the Arctic Fuse 3 Kodi skin repo and produce a Harbor remote-first nav/interaction spec

studies.howItWorks: Arctic Fuse 3's focus navigation is remote-first and D-pad-driven, built on Kodi's control routing system. Every focusable element (button, list, grouplist) has explicit `<onup>`, `<ondown>`, `<onleft>`, `<onright>` attributes that point to other control IDs or execute Kodi actions. The home screen uses a dual-panel layout: the left sidebar (ID 300, a button menu) acts as a hub switcher, while the content area (IDs 301, 400, 500, 601, 602) displays hierarchical content. Focus routing is context-aware—vertical mode (HomeSwitcher.Vertical setting) uses left/right to move between sidebar and content, while horizontal mode uses up/down. List controls (fixedlist, wraplist, panel, grouplist) auto-scroll to keep focused items visible, with a `scrolltime` delay (400ms quadratic tween) between focus moves. A spotlight area (ID 301, wraplist) wraps left/right and uses `Control.Move(301,-1/1)` for circular navigation. The framework layers focused and unfocused layouts with conditional visibility—when a control gains focus, a fade animation (200-300ms) swaps between two visual states. Default focus on window load is determined by `<defaultcontrol>` with an `always` flag, and focus memory is preserved using window properties and container metadata. Hard stops (top/bottom of content) route to external windows (ActivateWindow(1180)) rather than allowing escape; this enforces purposeful navigation boundaries.

keyBehaviors.detail: Each control has onup/ondown/onleft/onright attributes pointing to numeric control IDs (300=sidebar, 601=categories, 500=widgets) or Kodi commands. Onup from a widget goes to ID 601 (category list), ondown from 601 goes to the current widget (resolved via Container(601).ListItem.Property(widget_id)).

In vertical mode, onleft from content area ID 601 routes to sidebar ID 300 (SetFocus(300)); onright from 300 navigates to content (SetFocus(601) or SetProperty(Bumper.Direction,Right,Home) + ActivateWindow(1180) for fullscreen).

Spotlight (ID 301) is a wraplist—left/right arrows cycle through items via Control.Move(301,-1) and Control.Move(301,1), allowing circular navigation. Content rows (fixedlist) do not wrap; pressing down on the last row activates window 1180 (bumper UI) instead of looping.

Focus state is preserved using window properties (e.g., Container(601).CurrentItem) and skin strings (e.g., HomeSwitcher.Home.Toggle). On returning to a hub, the system checks these properties and restores the previous focused item via SetFocus($INFO[Container(601).ListItem.Property(widget_id)]).

List items have `<itemlayout>` (unfocused) and `<focusedlayout>` (focused). The focusedlayout contains two control groups with inverse fade animations—when Control.HasFocus(id) is true, one fades in (100%) while the other fades out (0%), creating a swap effect over 200ms.

Horizontal list controls use `<focusposition>2</focusposition>` to keep the focused item centered in view (item appears at index 2 of the visible window, typically 3-5 items visible). This applies to category selectors (ID 601) and button trays.

keyBehaviors.behavior: Conditional navigation based on layout orientation
keyBehaviors.detail: Onup/ondown from widgets checks Skin.HasSetting(HomeSwitcher.Vertical)—if true, up triggers a bumper direction property and window switch; if false, up goes directly to ID 300 (sidebar). This toggles between vertical stacking and horizontal side-by-side layouts.

keyBehaviors.behavior: Initial and return focus with default controls
keyBehaviors.detail: Home.xml and hub windows use `<defaultcontrol always='false'>300</defaultcontrol>` to set initial focus, with fallback to 400 if the sidebar is hidden. First-run logic (Action_FirstStart_HomeFocus) prioritizes spotlight (310) > category (601) > first visible widget (501+) > sidebar (300).

keyBehaviors.behavior: Movement constants for different item sizes
keyBehaviors.detail: List controls define a `<movement>` value (e.g., 6 for posters, 4 for cards, 3 for landscape, 2 for board) that controls how many items shift horizontally when focus moves left/right, keeping the list centered dynamically.

ID 602 is a grouplist (vertical) containing nested widget groups. Pressing down from ID 601 auto-focuses the first focusable child of the active widget group—Kodi's grouplist system handles this without explicit routing in each widget.

studies.howItWorks: Arctic Fuse 3 uses a layered animation system where scrolling containers employ `scrolltime` elements (300-400ms, quadratic tween) to smoothly move between items. Each item layout uses conditional fade/zoom animations to swap between focused and unfocused states instantly. The single Focus animation (Animation_FocusBounce) applies a coordinated zoom-scale and fade effect: zooming from 96.5% to 100% while simultaneously fading from 20% to 100% opacity, both over 150ms with sine tween inout easing, creating a "lift and brighten" effect. Movement between rows/sections triggers a directional bumper animation (slide 60ms, return slide 180ms with 80ms delay, sine tween) that provides haptic-like feedback. Dialog entries use zoom-in (400ms, back tween out) and slide-in animations (300-600ms, back or cubic tween). All animations use parametrized delays and conditions to create staggered, responsive behavior without blocking navigation.

keyBehaviors.detail: When an item gains focus via D-pad navigation, Animation_FocusBounce triggers: zoom 96.5→100% + fade 20→100% over 150ms (sine tween, inout easing). Uses center="auto" to scale from item center. Creates lift effect; no visual delay on focus gain itself.

All list containers use scrolltime element with 300-400ms duration (quadratic tween, applied to container movement). Easing varies by context: "out" for standard views, "inout" for seekbar. Movement values (1-7) control visible items before scrolling triggers.

Instead of animating the focused item, Arctic Fuse maintains two layout versions (itemlayout + focusedlayout) and cross-fades between them using conditional fade animations (fade 100→0 on unfocus, fade 100→0 on focus, both instant). Selected state visually swaps a SelectBox outline and changes text color via conditional diffuse effects.

Navigation between menu groups (Home hub) triggers Animation_Group_Bumper: quick initial slide (±20px, 60ms sine tween) followed by return slide to origin (20px → 0, 180ms sine, 80ms delay). Direction reverses based on nav direction (up/down/left/right).

Dialogs use two-stage animations: fade 0→100% (200-300ms) + zoom/slide from off-screen (400-600ms, back tween out). Delays are parameterized (200-400ms visible_delay, 400ms windowopen_delay) for staggered appearance.

Non-focus-based state changes (loading, expanded view) use Conditional animation type with parametrized duration, tween, and end values. Example: fade 100→$PARAM[end] (time=200, sine, out) triggers when Container.IsUpdating or custom expression evaluates.

Create React component `<FocusBounce>` using Framer Motion: `spring({ stiffness: 400, damping: 30 })` or manual keyframes over 150ms. Apply transform: scale(0.965)→scale(1) + opacity: 0.2→1 simultaneously. Trigger on spatial-nav focus callback via useEffect.

studies.howItWorks: Arctic Fuse 3 uses a hierarchical, grid-based sizing system anchored to a 1920x1080 (16:9) default design resolution, with all dimensions defined in pixel constants and font sizes following a pentatonic typographic scale (base 30px, ratio 2). The system scales responsively to other aspect ratios (4:3, 16:10, 17:9, 18:9, 19.5:9, 21:9) via aspect-ratio-specific constant overrides. Typography is based on Inter font with 7 semantic tiers plus decorative options; all font rendering uses linespacing multipliers (1.13–1.45×) quantized to 40px grid units for justified plot text. Component sizing is organized by view type (posters, cards, landscapes, icons) with consistent 40-120px padding/margins between items. Focus animations are geometric (96.5%→100% zoom + 20%→100% fade over 150ms) with ColorHighlight border rings at 20px border radius.

keyBehaviors.detail: Font sizes follow n=5, r=2, f0=30px formula: hint(15) → tiny(21) → mini(24) → main(28) → midi(32) → head(38) → huge(43). Doubles for large titles: main_x2(56), midi_x2(64), huge_x2(86). Inter-Regular, Inter-Bold, Inter-Light; optional Inter-Unicode for CJK. All fonts use calculated linespacing (1.13–1.455×) for readability at 10-foot TV viewing distance.

All major spacing values snap to 40px grid (view_pad: 80, view_top: 180, view_row: 510). Dialog buttons fixed at 320px width with 30px text offset; buttons scale to 520px for main dialogs. Padding inside containers: -3px to +40px depending on context.

Icons: 160×230px item / 200×270px layout, movement=8. Movement values control focus scroll smoothness (lower = slower); layout dimensions include padding/gap.

No bounce for board/banner items. Border rendered with ColorHighlight (theme-determined, typically bright accent).

Default 1920×1080; override files for 16:10 (1728×1080), 18:9 (2160×1080), etc. Movement and notification repositioning values change per ratio to maintain visual balance. Aspect-specific .xml files layer over base constants; no CSS media queries—pure XML conditions.

studies.howItWorks: Arctic Fuse opens context menus using Kodi's `Action(contextmenu)` remote action, which activates dialog windows (1170, 1172, or contextmenu dialog ID). Each dialog is an XML-based layout with D-pad navigation: items are arranged in lists or grids with `<onup>/<ondown>/<onleft>/<onright>` properties controlling focus routing. When focused, items show a different `<focusedlayout>` with highlight color and optional hint labels. The main options dialog has a side-panel layout (DialogSideMenu) with optional bottom tray of horizontal buttons. The context menu dialog for titles has a two-part layout: left side shows title artwork/metadata pulled from TMDbHelper properties, right side has a vertical list of action items (plot, wiki, related, trailers, etc.) with a scrollbar. Animations slide/bounce the dialog in/out. In Harbor, context menus are invoked via native JavaScript `contextmenu` event (right-click), displayed as portals outside the DOM tree. The "big" menu for titles is a full-screen modal overlay with a semi-transparent backdrop, a left preview card (300px), and a right action panel (440px, scrollable list). A spatial-nav system drives focus using arrow keys or D-pad, with geometric scoring (forward distance weighted 1:1, cross distance weighted 2:1) to find the nearest focusable element. Focus is scoped to the topmost open dialog. When the big context menu opens, the first action auto-focuses after 40ms (allowing remote users to navigate without initial button press). The small popup menu (for player/editable fields) uses fixed positioning near the mouse cursor.

keyBehaviors.detail: Arctic Fuse responds to `Action(contextmenu)` built-in Kodi action, mapping to the remote's context/options button. Dialog windows use `<onup>/<ondown>/<onleft>/<onright>` elements on list/button controls to explicitly route focus (e.g., `<ondown>SetFocus(7020)</ondown>`). Harbor uses native `document.addEventListener('contextmenu')` for right-click, and `addEventListener('keydown')` with arrow key mapping (ArrowUp/Down/Left/Right). Spatial-nav maintains scoping to the topmost dialog via `scopeRoot()` which queries `[role='dialog'], [aria-modal='true']` and filters visible dialogs.

Arctic Fuse uses strict containment: left/right arrows stay in the same row with hard stops at edges (no wrapping). Harbor's spatial-nav implements the same with `requireOverlap` flag in the `pick()` function—when true, left/right only consider elements in the same vertical band (checked via `Math.min(a.bottom, r.bottom) - Math.max(a.top, r.top)`). Up/down prefer the same column but fall back to any element in the next row if nothing aligns.

Arctic Fuse's context menu for titles uses `Part_Dialog_Backing` with `Dimension_DialogFlipMenu` (if artwork shown) or `Dimension_DialogMiniMenu`. Left group `Size_LeftGroup_Large` contains `DialogPlot_Artwork` (poster image fetched from `$INFO[Window(Home).Property(TMDbHelper.ListItem.base_poster)]`). Right group `Size_RightGroup_Large` contains header (title label, close button) and body with the list (ID 6000, vertical list, width 100px, height 700px). Harbor mirrors this: preview card (300px, aspect-ratio 2/3, hidden on mobile) on left, action panel (440px, flex flex-col) on right, both wrapped in a full-screen overlay with `bg-black/55 backdrop-blur-sm` and `animate-popover-in`.

keyBehaviors.behavior: Automatic focus to first item on menu open
keyBehaviors.detail: Arctic Fuse uses `<onload>AlarmClock(refocus,SetFocus(7021),00:00,silent)</onload>` to defer focus (AlarmClock can run async actions). Harbor uses `useEffect` with `window.setTimeout(..., 40)` to focus the first action button (marked with `data-cm-first` attribute) after 40ms, allowing the portal to render before focus shift, which prevents visual jank and lets remote users immediately navigate.

Arctic Fuse's options dialog uses `<grouplist>` with `<pagecontrol>` linked to a scrollbar. The list has dynamic `<onup>` routing that walks up through visible items (e.g., `SetFocus(1020)` if visible, else `SetFocus(1019)`, ..., fall back to `SetFocus(1001)`). Arctic shows page info via `Part_ItemPage` displaying 'X / Y'. Harbor wraps items in a `flex flex-1 flex-col overflow-y-auto` div with `[scrollbar-width:none]` to hide the native scrollbar. No explicit pagination UI; it's implicit in the scrollable height (max height fills remaining space after header).

keyBehaviors.behavior: Conditional item visibility and dynamic labels
keyBehaviors.detail: Arctic Fuse items use `<visible>` blocks with Kodi conditions (e.g., `<visible>Window.IsVisible(videos)</visible>`) and dynamic labels via `$INFO[...]` and `$VAR[...]` (e.g., `<label>$VAR[Label_SortMethod]</label>`). Harbor uses React state and conditional rendering: items are built in JavaScript, and `onClick` handlers close the menu after performing action (e.g., `const handleDetails = () => { openMeta(meta); done(); }`). Each BigAction has id, icon, label, onClick, and optional `accent` flag.

Arctic Fuse doesn't show explicit Escape handling in the main dialog XML; it relies on Kodi's dialog system (Back button/menu key closes). Harbor's context-menu useEffect listens for `e.key === 'Escape'` and calls `close()`. It also closes on scroll or window resize (via `addEventListener('scroll', onScroll, true)`).

keyBehaviors.behavior: Accent/highlight for special actions and state indicators
keyBehaviors.detail: Arctic Fuse uses color schemes: unfocused items use `color_nf` (e.g., 'dialog_fg_70'), focused items use `$VAR[ColorSelected]`. Harbor uses a `accent` boolean on BigAction: true items get `bg-accent/90 text-white` class, false get `text-ink` with `hover:bg-raised`. Watchlist/favorite actions are marked with `accent: true` to highlight their importance (watchlist toggle is line 225-230).

keyBehaviors.behavior: Bottom tray of media controls (player context)
keyBehaviors.detail: Arctic Fuse includes `ButtonMenu_MediaTray` (a horizontal fixedlist ID 6099 with `orientation=horizontal, focusposition=2, movement=2, height=100px`) containing Play, Previous, Next, Fullscreen, etc. Tray only appears when playing (`<include condition='Player.HasMedia'>`). Harbor shows player-specific actions inline in the small popup menu when `playerActions` is truthy, adding Fullscreen and Download items with separator.

keyBehaviors.behavior: Customizable tile/button layouts (2, 3, or 4 per row)
keyBehaviors.detail: Arctic Fuse uses `Skin.String(OptionsTiles.Layout)` to select layout: `ButtonMenu_Layout_Two`, `_Three`, or `_Four`. Each layout wraps buttons in `ButtonMenu_LayoutGroup_Single` (vertical stack), `_Double` (two buttons side-by-side with `width=50%` left/right), organizing them as a grid. Harbor does not have this—actions are always a single vertical list. However, Harbor's preview card (left side) could be styled as a tile-like component.

studies.dimension: tmdbhelper: Info dialogs, ratings aggregation, similar/recommended content, cast/crew widgets, Trakt sync/watchlist/favorites overlays
studies.howItWorks: Arctic Fuse uses tmdbhelper (a Kodi plugin) to enrich listitem metadata at focus/selection time. When a user focuses a video card, the plugin fetches TMDB data and populates Window(Home).Property(TMDbHelper.*) properties. The skin then conditionally renders: (1) an expanded info panel on the left showing ratings (Trakt, IMDb, RT, Metacritic, TMDb, MDBList, Letterboxd), metadata (duration, premiere, status, awards), and styled pill-tags for video quality (4K/FHD/HD), HDR type, audio channels; (2) a right-side info dialog (window 1190) with poster, title, plot, and scrollable horizontal widget rows for Cast, Collection, Studio, Year, Seasons, Episodes, Director, Writer, Creator, Starring actors, Crew lists, Trailers, Reviews, and Recommendations. Each widget row pulls from TMDbHelper plugin endpoints (plugin://plugin.video.themoviedb.helper/?info=cast|collection|recommendations|stars_in_both|crew_in_both, etc.). Trakt integration adds: (a) watchlist/favorites rank properties (watchlist_rank, favorites_rank) rendered as 40x40px overlay icons (top-right, slide 48px down if both present); (b) a Trakt button in the info dialog's action tray that calls RunScript(plugin.video.themoviedb.helper,sync_trakt,tmdb_id=X,type=movie|tv,cache_refresh) to sync local and Trakt state; (c) Trakt auth check via Window(Home).Property(TMDbHelper.TraktIsAuth). All data sourcing is deferred (only fetched when dialog has focus) and cached client-side by the plugin. Context menu exposes Plot, Wiki, Related Lists, Trailer, and Add-to-Favorites via Dialog_DialogContextMenu.xml, which reads base properties like TMDbHelper.ListItem.base_plot, base_poster, base_dbtype.

keyBehaviors.detail: Focus on a video card → onload fires ClearProperty(Background.HideArtwork) in window 1190 → poster slides in from right (160px, 400ms cubic-out easing, 200ms delay) → info panel slides in from left (160px, 400ms) → widgets group below fades in (1000ms delay default). Dialog animates on unfocus with reverse slide-out.

Up/down d-pad scrolls vertical grouplist of widget rows. Each widget is a horizontal list (List_Poster_Row or List_Landscape_Row) with SetFocus triggers that set Window.Property(CurrentID) to manage which widget is active. Widgets with altvisible show only when CurrentID != BaseID, preventing duplicate display when focused.

Info_Meta_Ratings conditionally renders 1-3 rating pills per row (40px tall), spaced 12px apart, showing Metacritic (%), RT User/Critic, Trakt (/10), IMDb (with Top250 if present), TMDb (/10), MDBList (%), Letterboxd. Each pulls from Window(Home).Property(TMDbHelper.ListItem.XXX_Rating). Visible only if value is non-empty and enabled via Skin.String(CustomRating.Movies/TVShows.Item0X). Pill styling: 60px wide auto-text, 2a2a2a text on color-diffuse bg (90% alpha).

Object_TraktOverlays renders two 40x40 icon groups (top-right of posters) with 12px margin. Watchlist icon visible if Skin.HasSetting(Indicator.TraktWatchlist) + !String.IsEmpty(ListItem.Property(watchlist_rank)). Favorites icon slides down 48px only if both present, conditional animation time=400ms. Icons are 40x40 with textures flags/color/trakt/watchlist.png and favorite.png.

Trakt button in dialog (id=1172) closes dialog then fires $VAR[Action_Sync], which runs RunScript(plugin.video.themoviedb.helper,sync_trakt,tmdb_id=X,type=movie|tv,season=Y,episode=Z,cache_refresh). Button only visible if !String.IsEmpty(Window(Home).Property(TMDbHelper.TraktIsAuth)) + Exp_TMDbHelper_IsData.

Recommendations widget (id=16) populates from Path_VideoInfo_OnlineRecommendations, which is plugin://plugin.video.themoviedb.helper/?info=recommendations&nextpage=false&cacheonly=true&length=1&tmdb_type=movie&tmdb_id=X. Only loads when widget container (ControlGroup 5000) has focus, triggered by ondown from main panel.

Cast widgets pull from Path_VideoInfo_OnlineCast (plugin://plugin.video.themoviedb.helper/?info=cast&...). Creator/Director/Writer crew items hardcoded as 10 items max from Window(Home).Property(TMDbHelper.ListItem.Creator.1.Name) etc., with filter support for department/job (e.g., filter_key=job&filter_value=Director). First actor/director also shown in a 'starring' widget.

Dialog_DialogContextMenu.xml has no explicit Trakt item (unlike Wikipedia/Plot/Trailer), but related_lists item calls RunScript(plugin.video.themoviedb.helper,related_lists,query=X,tmdb_type=Y) to show Trakt lists. The manage watchlist/favorites is done via the main info dialog Trakt button, not the context menu.

studies.howItWorks: Arctic Fuse 3's info dialog is a D-pad-driven detail view with conditional focus routing and layered visibility toggles. The layout has a fixed right sidebar (poster + details panel) and a main content area (title + plot + ratings). Below is a widget carousel. When the widget carousel (ID 5000) gains focus, the entire main content panel (zoom 100%->85%, fade 100%->0%) and slides the widgets up 880px, creating a two-view modal flip. The action buttons (Play, Trailer, Watchlist, etc.) are a horizontal grouplist with 40px gaps. All focus transitions are smooth (300-400ms cubic/quadratic/sine tweens). The focus routing is conditional: navigation changes based on whether the item is a movie vs person vs episode, enabling context-aware button availability and navigation targets.

keyBehaviors.detail: Horizontal grouplist (ID 9000) with 5 conditional buttons: Play (ID 4001), Action1-3 (IDs 4002-4004), Options (ID 4005). Buttons auto-size to text width with 40px horizontal gap. Focus ring is underline that appears beneath text. Font is font_main_bold, 57px tall. Button text color is dialog_fg_30 (unfocused, ~30% opacity) to dialog_fg_100 (focused, bright). On focus, button icon slides up 10px and text color brightens. ondown from here goes to details panel (9100) or widgets (5000). onup loops back to title (8000).

keyBehaviors.behavior: Main content zoom-out on widget focus
keyBehaviors.detail: When widget grouplist (ID 5000) gains focus, the entire main content area (poster + details + title + plot) undergoes simultaneous animations: zoom 100%->85% + fade 100%->0% in 300ms using sine easing out. This is managed by Dialog_Main_Group visibility condition: !ControlGroup(5000).HasFocus(). Reverse animation (85%->100%, 0%->100%) triggers on 300ms sine easing in with 200ms delay when focus leaves widgets.

Widget grouplist (ID 5000) occupies top:880, bottom:-880 (total 1080p below main content). When it gains focus, animation slides it up by 880px in 400ms using quadratic tween, bringing it to fill the screen. When focus leaves, it slides back down. The animation is reversible=false and only activates on Conditional animation type. This creates the flip-between-views effect.

Up arrow: varies by current location. From action buttons->title; from details->action buttons; from widgets->title. Down arrow: from title->action buttons; from action buttons->details or widgets. Left/Right: cycles through buttons in action row (grouplist native horizontal navigation). Back button: from any widget->title (explicit onback routing). Conditional branches: if ListItem.Property(tmdb_type)==person, onleft/onright from title activate window 1113 (external); otherwise route to action buttons (4001).

Buttons 4002-4004 appear/disappear based on $INFO[Container(4900).ListItemAbsolute(N).Label] (a hidden list of configured actions). Movies get 3 buttons (often Trailer, Trakt, Plot); TV shows get different buttons (Watch, Unwatched, Trakt). Buttons render only if label is not empty. On click, they SetFocus(4900,N,absolute), Action(select), then SetFocus back to button, ensuring focus returns after action.

Vertical grouplist (ID 9100) at right: 480px wide, right-aligned. Contains genres, directors, cast, etc. onup goes to action buttons (9000). onleft/onright connects to main details grouplist (9100). No horizontal scrolling; it's a static details column next to the dynamic main content.

On dialog open: Main content slides left 160px (start position) with cubic out easing over 400ms + 200ms delay. Poster slides right 160px over same duration. Widgets slide down 160px. Creates a coordinated entrance where everything moves inward from edges. On close, same animations reverse.

Focus fade animation easing: sine out/in
Window entrance animation easing: cubic out

studies.howItWorks: Arctic Fuse 3's search uses a remote-first, spatially-driven focus model with four distinct layers: (1) a text input field at the top center (Control 3000), (2) a horizontal on-screen keyboard wraplist below it (Control 301) displaying A-Z letters plus underscore and backspace, (3) discovery/results content displayed as horizontal poster rows (Containers 501, 601, 602), and (4) a horizontal widget switcher/filter bar (Control 600) for selecting result types. Focus flows vertically between layers via onup/ondown handlers and horizontally within layers. Letter selection in the keyboard immediately updates the search query; the input focus automatically advances focus down to results on pressing down. All transitions use fade animations and smooth scrolling. The design prioritizes remote D-pad usability with explicit navigation targets.

keyBehaviors.detail: From search input (ID 3000), pressing down focuses the keyboard (ID 301) immediately below. Input field shows outlined backing panel when unfocused (menu.png border 40px), highlight panel when focused (ColorHighlight white border).

Horizontal wraplist (ID 301) contains 27 items (A-Z, underscore, backspace). Each letter on click: focuses input, executes script to append letter to query, returns focus to keyboard. Focus position is always item 7 (centered). Selected letter shown bold with white underline bar (scrollbar texture, height 10px, 5px from bottom).

Pressing down from keyboard (ID 301) routes to results (ID 601 in combined mode, ID 602 in standard). Container 601 is discover widget; containers in group 500 show as horizontal poster rows. When results focus gained, widget switcher (ID 600) appears below for filtering.

Poster rows use fixedlist horizontal layout, 6 visible items per row (view_poster_movement=6). Each poster is 217.14px wide x 310px tall. Items arranged with 80px horizontal offset (view_offx), 80px padding around. Scroll animation: 400ms quadratic tween. Focused item shown with white highlight box (border 20px).

Below results, horizontal filter tabs (ID 600) let user switch between movies/shows/actors/etc. Each tab (Container 601 items) shows result count. Only visible when results container has items. onup from switcher goes to results; onup from results goes to keyboard.

When all result containers are empty (Container 601 NumItems=0 AND Container 602 NumItems=0), displays centered text at top 280px with fade-in animation (400ms delay 200ms). Message includes two lines: bold title, description text.

All visible/hidden transitions use fade effect: fade in 0→100% over 400ms with 200ms delay; fade out 100→0% over 200ms. List item focus uses conditional fade toggle showing focused layout (bold, highlight) vs unfocused layout.

When navigating between major sections (input to keyboard, keyboard to results), a subtle bounce animation plays: 60ms slide out, then 180ms slide back with sine tween. Creates tactile confirmation without disrupting focus flow.

studies.howItWorks: Arctic Fuse 3's home navigation is a multi-layer remote-first system built on Kodi's XML event model. The primary interaction hub is Control 300 (the main menu button), which sits at the top and manages focus routing to a hidden horizontal menu list (Container 399). When users press UP from this menu, Kodi activates Window 1181 (Submenu dialog), displaying a vertical list of contextual menu items specific to the current hub (Home, Movies, TV, etc.). Window 1182 (Topmenu_Overlay) is a persistent overlay showing the current menu switcher with icons and highlight states. The system uses "Bumper.Direction" properties to track D-pad intent before executing complex multi-step focus routing. Navigation patterns differ between horizontal mode (menu at top, widgets below) and vertical mode (menu at left, widgets to the right). All transitions use conditional animations triggered by property changes and container states. The spotlight feature (Container 301) is a horizontally-scrolling carousel that slides down/up based on focus state and visibility. Widget rows (Containers 5xx) are arranged vertically in a grouplist (Container 602), and users navigate between rows, the widget category switcher (Container 601), and back to the menu using UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT arrows.

keyBehaviors.detail: Central focus hub with left/right navigation cycling through menu items via Container 399. Each item (Search, Home, 1101-1108) is a property-based action trigger. When focused, shows full-screen highlighted state with background overlay fade.

Triggered by UP or INFO key from menu. Opens a modal dialog containing a vertical list (Container 80XX) of submenu items. Has onload logic to set focus based on active window (Home=7000, 1101=7001, etc). Closes via LEFT/RIGHT navigation or BACK key. Width 560px, height 880px with conditional repositioning for vertical mode.

Persistent transparent overlay showing current menu switcher state. Displays horizontal menu in normal mode or vertical sidebar icons in vertical mode. Fades in/out (200-400ms) based on HomeSwitcher.IsVisible property. Shows icon highlights based on active window and current control state.

Horizontal wraplist with quadratic 400ms scrolltime. Auto-hides menu switcher via fade animation when focused. Slides down 260px or up based on focus and menu visibility. Contains circular button controls for play/info actions. Only visible when container has items.

Vertical grouplist layout with multiple display modes (Standard, Wall, Combined). Focus on widgets clears the menu switcher visibility and hides background overlay. UP from widgets returns to menu or spotlight. DOWN goes to bottom menu overlay (1180). Rows auto-scroll left/right through categories via Container 601 switcher.

Complex navigation uses SetProperty(Bumper.Direction,Up/Down/Left/Right,Home) before opening dialogs. This stores intent to be consumed by dialog logic. Prevents focus loss during dialog transitions and enables multi-key sequences to execute atomically.

Skin.HasSetting(HomeSwitcher.Vertical) switches entire layout. Horizontal: menu at top, full widgets below. Vertical: menu as 80px left sidebar with icons + gaps, main content slides right when menu visible, submenu panel slides from left with repositioning animations based on item count.

All visible/hidden animations keyed to properties and container states. E.g., Home_Switcher fades/slides based on HomeSwitcher.IsVisible property. Spotlight slides based on ControlGroup(310).HasFocus() and Window.Property(TMDbHelper.WidgetContainer) values. No explicit animation triggers—all driven by state expressions.

Modal <dialog> or overlay div with conditional visibility class. Menu items rendered as focusable <button> or <li> elements in a <ul>. Spatial-nav tracks focus within this list. CSS transitions replace animation blocks: fade 200-400ms ease-out, slide from top -48px with cubic-bezier back easing. SetFocus(n) → spatial-nav.focus(id).

Fixed position <aside> or <nav> overlay (z-index >= 10). Children are icon <button> elements. Visibility tied to state variable (Redux/Context) tracking HomeSwitcher.IsVisible property. CSS classes toggle fade/slide animations: opacity 0→1 400ms, transform slide-in 400ms cubic. Different layout (flexDirection: column for vertical, row for horizontal).

Horizontal scrollable <div> with overflow-x and snap-scroll or smooth-scroll CSS. Renders carousel items as cards. Spatial-nav moves left/right with smooth JS scrolling over 400ms (requestAnimationFrame). Use transform: translateX() for hardware-accelerated scroll. scrollBehavior: smooth replaces quadratic tween. Focus on spotlight triggers state change to update sidebar/menu overlay visibility.

Grid or vertical flex layout of row containers. Each row is a scrollable horizontal section of cards. Container 602 is CSS grid with auto-rows or gap-based layout. Spatial-nav handles UP/DOWN through rows, LEFT/RIGHT within rows. Onfocus handlers update TMDbHelper.WidgetContainer property (use Context/Redux) to trigger secondary info panel visibility.

State machine using Redux/Context or local component state. When dialog opens, dispatch action storing direction intent. Dialog onload checks intent and routes focus: if Up + Home visible → focus submenu item 7000, etc. This replaces Kodi's property system with React state & useEffect() hooks.

CSS transitions and keyframes with var() for timing/easing. Main transitions: fade 400ms cubic-bezier(0.32, 0, 0.67, 0), slide 400ms cubic-bezier(0.68, -0.55, 0.265, 1.55) [back easing], spotlight scroll 400ms cubic-bezier(0.33, 0.66, 0.66, 1) [quadratic]. Use Framer Motion or CSS modules to apply conditional animations keyed to boolean state flags.

CSS media-query-like state or feature flag in Context. When active, apply layout transform: CSS grid switches from 1 column to 2 columns (sidebar + content). Sidebar becomes 80px fixed width, main content area flex-grows. Toggle via settings panel action, persist to localStorage/DB, trigger full layout re-render.

useNavigate() from React Router or custom history hook. onback translates to browser back button or a dispatch(closeDialog()). In spatial-nav, map BACK key to exit submenu dialog and restore focus to Control 300.

CSS Grid gap: 20px, button size width: 80px; height: 80px. Icon nested inside: width: 40px; height: 40px; margin: auto (center via flexbox). Border or borderRadius applied via Tailwind or SCSS mixin matching Kodi design (typically borderRadius: 4-8px for sharp, modern look).

navPrinciples: Remote-first, every focusable is purposeful: Harbor already routes all arrow keys through src/lib/spatial-nav.ts (geometric pick(): forward + cross*2 scoring, requireOverlap for straight row travel). Keep that engine as the single source of truth; do not sprinkle onKeyDown handlers per component. Arctic mirror: explicit onup/ondown/onleft/onright on every control, but Harbor gets it for free from geometry.

Rows are hard stops, columns fall through: left/right end-of-row is a dead end (Arctic non-wrapping fixedlist), up/down always finds the next row or scrolls (spatial-nav.ts lines 144-152). This is already correct and should be preserved.

The focused item lives in a stable zone, not the edge: Arctic keeps the focused item at focusposition=2 (centered) and scrolls the strip under it. Harbor currently uses scrollIntoView({inline:'nearest'}) which parks items at the edge. Move to inline:'center' for horizontal rows so the strip glides under a fixed cursor.

Focus is always visible and always animated: Arctic never teleports focus; every move is a 300-400ms tween plus a 150ms lift on the item. Harbor must give the same tactile feedback so a remote user can see focus travel.

Boundaries are meaningful, not silent: Left on a deep page exits to sidebar/back (already in spatial-nav via data-harbor-page='deep' + Backspace-to-sidebar); a hard stop should give a bump, not nothing (Arctic bumper spring).

Entering the sidebar lands on the current tab (already implemented, lines 154-163); entering a page lands on a deliberate default control (Arctic <defaultcontrol>), and returning to a page restores the last-focused item (Arctic Container.CurrentItem memory) — this is the main missing piece.

Modals own focus: scopeRoot() already traps focus in the topmost [role=dialog]/[aria-modal] (lines 52-58); context-menu and search-overlay already set these attributes. Keep every overlay dialog-scoped.
Keep Harbor's look. Adopt Arctic's timings and interaction model (centering, bounce, wrap, memory), not its skin. Search in particular: change only its remote-drivability, never its visual layout.
animationSpec: Add easing tokens to :root in src/index.css alongside the existing --ease-out (cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1)) and --ease-in-out (cubic-bezier(0.65,0,0.35,1)): --ease-quad: cubic-bezier(0.455,0.03,0.515,0.955) (Arctic quadratic scrolltime); --ease-sine-inout: cubic-bezier(0.445,0.05,0.55,0.95) (Arctic focus bounce); --ease-back-out: cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1) (Arctic dialog/back tween).

Focus bounce (Arctic Animation_FocusBounce, 150ms): @keyframes harbor-focus-bounce { 0% { transform: scale(0.965); } 100% { transform: scale(1); } } applied on .harbor-card-focus:focus-visible for 150ms var(--ease-sine-inout). Keep Harbor's existing inset ring + lift; do NOT copy Arctic's 20%->100% opacity fade (that was a two-layer swap Harbor doesn't need). Onscreen keyboard keys already do focus-visible:scale-110 — align them to the same 150ms token.

Row focus-scroll (Arctic scrolltime 400ms quadratic): when spatial-nav moves focus within a horizontal row, animate the strip over ~400ms with --ease-quad. Reuse row.tsx glideTo() (already a cubic ease-out, 280-620ms) but drive it from focus, targeting the centered position; clamp focus-driven glides to 300-400ms so remote travel feels consistent.

Dialog / info panel entrance (Arctic 400ms cubic-out, 200ms delay, 160px slide): reuse existing animate-modal-in (480ms var(--ease-out)) for context/info dialogs; for the two-stage title info dialog use scale 0.965->1 + opacity 0->1 over 400ms var(--ease-back-out).

Bumper spring at hard stops (Arctic 60ms out + 180ms back, ±20px, sine): @keyframes harbor-bump-x { 0% { transform: translateX(0);} 25% { transform: translateX(var(--bump,8px)); } 100% { transform: translateX(0);} } total ~240ms var(--ease-sine-inout); fire on the active element when pick() returns null. Scale Arctic's 1080-canvas 20px down to ~8px web px.

Context menu open: keep animate-popover-in (220ms var(--ease-out), already in index.css line 478) and the existing deferred first-item focus (context-menu.tsx line 168-169, setTimeout ~40ms to [data-cm-first]) — this already matches Arctic's AlarmClock deferred SetFocus.
Respect prefers-reduced-motion: wrap bounce/bump/glide in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) and fall back to instant focus + scrollIntoView({behavior:'auto'}).
sizingSpec: Treat Arctic's numbers as ratios, not pixels: Arctic is authored on a fixed 1920x1080 canvas; Harbor is fluid web/Tauri. Convert Arctic dimensions to rem/relative or keep the proportions, don't hardcode 1080-canvas px.

Focus ring: Arctic uses a 20px border ring with -3px inset on the 1080 canvas. Harbor already lands this correctly with inset box-shadow 0 0 0 2px accent@60% on .harbor-card-ring (index.css lines 713-721) plus outline:none on .harbor-card-focus:focus-visible. Keep as-is; do not widen to Arctic's literal 20px.

Center-focus offset: emulate Arctic focusposition=2 via scroll padding. Rows already use scroll-ps-5 scroll-pe-5 (20px, gap-5). For centered focus, scroll the focused card to inline:'center' rather than adding large scroll padding — cheaper and keeps the current visual gaps.

Context menu big panel: Harbor's 440px action panel + 300px preview card + 52px BigItem rows + 24px padding + 6px gaps already match Arctic's DialogContextMenu two-panel model (right list ~ Size_RightGroup, left artwork ~ DialogPlot_Artwork). No change needed — this dimension is already faithful.
Small popup menu: Harbor 220px wide, 36px (h-9) items — keep. Arctic's media tray (100px items, focusposition=2, horizontal) maps to Harbor's inline player actions; no separate tray needed.

Movement/visible-count: Arctic movement values (posters=6, cards=4, landscape=3, icons=8) control how many items shift per keypress. Harbor rows scroll one card per focus move with centering, which is the modern web equivalent; only adopt a multi-item jump if a row is very dense. Arrow-button scroll already pages by clientWidth (row.tsx line 305).
Search layout sizing is frozen: the onscreen keyboard (h-14 keys, w-14 / w-[92px] wide keys, 6-col grid, gap-2.5) and overlay stay visually identical. Only nav wiring changes.

prioritizedTasks.title: Center the focused card in horizontal rows (focusposition=2)
prioritizedTasks.whatToDo: After best.focus(), choose scroll alignment by axis: for left/right moves inside a horizontal scroller use best.scrollIntoView({block:'nearest', inline:'center', behavior:'smooth'}); keep inline:'nearest' for vertical. Detect the horizontal case by checking the nearest scrollable-x ancestor (mirror scrollableAncestor() with overflowX). This makes the strip glide under a fixed focus cursor instead of parking cards at the viewport edge.
prioritizedTasks.mirrors: Arctic focusposition=2 keeping the focused item centered while the fixedlist scrolls under it (ButtonMenu.xml line 66)

prioritizedTasks.title: Add the 150ms focus-bounce to cards

Keep the existing inset ring + hover lift untouched so Harbor's look is preserved; this only adds the tactile 'lift' on remote focus. prioritizedTasks.title: Per-page default focus + focus memory on back

Default stays hard-stop (no wrap) to preserve current row behavior; opt-in only for spotlight/hero carousels.

prioritizedTasks.title: Boundary bump feedback
prioritizedTasks.area: src/lib/spatial-nav.ts (move(), the no-best branch) + src/index.css (@keyframes harbor-bump-x/-y)
prioritizedTasks.whatToDo: When pick() finds no target and it is a genuine hard stop (left/right end of row, or top/bottom with nothing to scroll), add a one-shot bump class to the active element (±8px, ~240ms, var(--ease-sine-inout)) and remove on animationend. Gives the Arctic tactile confirmation that you hit an edge instead of silent nothing.

openQuestions: Center-focus vs edge-focus: Arctic centers the focused card (focusposition=2). This is a noticeable feel change from Harbor's current edge parking. Do you want full centering, or a softer 'keep 1-2 cards of lead-in' offset so the row start doesn't jump on the first card?
Wrap navigation scope: should hero/spotlight carousels wrap (circular) like Arctic's wraplist, or stay hard-stop like every other row for consistency? I lean opt-in wrap only for the hero.
Focus memory persistence: restore last-focused item within a session only (in-memory WeakMap), or persist across app restarts (sessionStorage/DB)? Arctic keeps it per-session via window properties.

Bounce intensity: Arctic's 96.5%->100% is subtle. On Harbor's already-lifting cards, do you want the scale bounce on top of the lift, or the lift alone with just the ring? I can ship it subtle and you tune.

Detail/info zoom-out (task 8) is the heaviest and most 'Arctic-looking' change. Confirm you want the header dim-and-shrink when focus enters the widgets area, or keep the detail view static to stay closer to Harbor's current look.
